target.name: Polar Semiconductor
target.founder: Independent spinout of Control Data (2024 reformation)

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Growth → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $700M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Chips & Semiconductors (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 14 same-sector peers exist in Chips & Semiconductors, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Polar Semiconductor is a Growth-stage spinout in the Chips & Semiconductors sector, formed in 2024 as an independent entity originating from Control Data. The company has raised $700M+ including state support. The broader semiconductor peer set includes 3 public comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private firms such as Cerebras, PsiQuantum, and Etched, though few share identical stage and scale characteristics.

Only one valuation method proved applicable to this analysis. Stage-Progression methodology, using the $700M total raised as a proxy base value and applying a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical for Growth-stage advancement, yields $840M. Peer-Median triangulation could not be applied due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within the narrow Chips & Semiconductors cohort—while 14 same-sector peers exist, none of the filtered subsets met the minimum threshold of 3 datapoints. Public-Comp Anchoring could not be executed because target revenue is not available; the public semiconductor universe trades at a 20.3x median P/S, but revenue-absent private companies cannot be priced on this basis.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $714M to $966M, centered on $840M. This range reflects limited methodological coverage; key uncertainties include the absence of direct peer multiples, undisclosed revenue metrics, and market comparability across the nascent frontier-chip landscape. Results should be treated as preliminary pending disclosure of operational metrics or narrower peer identification.
